l4_limitations: cost basis is census-declared per-loop figures; repo contains no billing-account evidence to re-derive them — Cloudflare Workers pricing is often per-account, so real deletion savings may be less than the attributed $20/mo
session-embedded LLM costs are unmetered by design (accounting_note convention) and excluded from cap arithmetic

task_results.1_per_loop_attribution: 38 loops attributed by value class: REVENUE 3 loops $5.00 · GUARD 5 loops $0.00 · META 22 loops $10.00 · NONE 8 loops $20.00 — full table in artifact
task_results.2_reconciliation: PASS — derived class totals equal census costs_usd_month exactly (REVENUE 5.00 / GUARD 0 / META 10.00 / NONE 20.00)
task_results.3_none_class_spend: All $20/mo of NONE spend sits in exactly 4 Cloudflare loops at $5 each, every one with zero receipts EVER: cf_brain_chat_v1, cf_gateway_ops_v1, cf_gateway_heartbeat_v1, trustfield_loops_preview. Critical cross-check vs M02: the first three were silently DEREGISTERED from the automation registry after the census — off the books but, per census billing attribution, still deployed and still billing. Deregistration is not decommissioning.
task_results.4_governor_computability: NOT ENFORCEABLE TODAY. (a) Monthly cap ($1,500/mo pre-revenue, router rules): no monthly spend-rollup receipt exists anywhere; execution receipts carry per-run cost but nothing aggregates them; session-embedded LLM spend is unmetered — cap breach is undetectable from repo evidence. (b) THROTTLED_ROI (LS-078, trigger: >30% NONE): guard is w0_status PENDING, receipt target has zero receipts, and the 30% threshold is ambiguous — by loop COUNT the fleet is at 21.1% (8/38, under), by COST it is at 57.1% ($20/$35, over). Missing inputs: monthly spend aggregator receipt, metered session-cost feed, count-vs-cost threshold definition, LS-078 implementation.
task_results.5_kill_throttle_candidates_no_action: Ranked, ZERO action: #1 cf_gateway_ops_v1 ($5, deregistered ghost) · #2 cf_gateway_heartbeat_v1 ($5, deregistered ghost) · #3 cf_brain_chat_v1 ($5, deregistered ghost) · #4 trustfield_loops_preview ($5, still registered, zero receipts). All four exceed the 14-day NONE standing rule (17+ days) — retirement proposals are DUE under the census's own law; the kill itself is founder-gated. Secondary (zero spend recovery, registry hygiene only): mac_launchd_brain_loop_autorun_v1, gh_actions_noos_release_gate_v1, gh_actions_noos_publish_pypi_v1, gh_gateway_railway_deadman_v1.

findings.finding: $20/mo (57% of all attributed loop spend) burns in 4 zero-receipt Cloudflare loops; 3 of them are deregistered ghosts — invisible to the registry yet still billing
findings.next_pointer: founder-gated retirement packet for the 4 loops, preceded by a Cloudflare-side existence/billing check to pin exact savings

findings.finding: Spend governor is paper-only: neither the $1,500/mo cap nor THROTTLED_ROI is computable from existing receipts; count-vs-cost ambiguity flips today's verdict (21% vs 57%)
findings.next_pointer: packet: monthly spend-rollup receipt motor + LS-078 implementation + founder definition of the 30% basis
